summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that a malicious VSIX with path traversal entries in its zip file names causes the code-marketplace to write files outside the designated extensions directory. The verify.sh script builds the marketplace from source, generates a malicious VSIX, adds it via the CLI, and confirms the proof file was written to /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt which is outside the /tmp/extensions directory.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause a crafted VSIX to be extracted with path traversal entries, resulting in a proof file being written to /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt which is outside the designated extensions directory at /tmp/extensions.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the code-marketplace binary from source in a Docker container, generated a malicious VSIX with entry names containing ../../../../ sequences, executed 'code-marketplace add /tmp/malicious.vsix --extensions-dir /tmp/extensions', and the proof file was successfully created at /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt containing the marker string EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF_OF_CONCEPT_SUCCESS.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks for the existence of /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt after the add command completes. It verifies the file contains the expected proof marker string 'EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF_OF_CONCEPT_SUCCESS'. This distinguishes a real path traversal from a normal extraction where files would only appear under /tmp/extensions/.
evidence_produced_by_run: The marketplace CLI reported 'Unpacked evil-publisher.test-extension@1.0.0 to /tmp/extensions/evil-publisher/test-extension/1.0.0' confirming extraction occurred.
The proof file /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/pwned.txt exists outside the extensions directory /tmp/extensions.
The proof file contains the marker string EVOHUNT_ZIP_SLIP_PROOF_OF_CONCEPT_SUCCESS.
A second proof file was written to /tmp/evohunt-zipslip-proof/deep/nested.txt demonstrating traversal to a nested path.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
